name: Ham Phyllo Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Chopped fully cooked smoked ham
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse bean sprouts under running cold water; drain.
2. Cook and stir ham, onion in a 10-inch skillet until onion is tender. Stir in bean sprouts, spinach, water chestnuts, and gingerroot; cook and stir for 2 minutes. Stir in cornstarch and soy sauce.
3. Cut stack of phyllo leaves lengthwise into halves. Cut each half crosswise into thirds to make 30 squares, about 5 1/2 x 5 1/2 inches. Cover squares with waxed paper, then with damp towel to prevent them from drying out.
4. Preheat oven to 350°F.
5. For each roll, use 2 phyllo squares. Place about 2 tbsp ham mixture slightly below center of square. Fold corner closest to filling over filling, tucking point under filling. Fold in and overlap the opposite corners. Roll up; place seam side down on greased cookie sheet. Repeat with remaining phyllo squares.
6. Brush rolls with 1 tbsp margarine. Bake until golden brown, about 25 minutes.
Do-ahead Tip: Before baking, place rolls on greased cookie sheet. Cover tightly with plastic wrap. Refrigerate up to 24 hours. Brush rolls with margarine. Bake in 350°F oven until golden brown, about 25 minutes.
